message:
deps: bump sha2 0.10.8 -> 0.11.0

Major bump (digest 0.10 -> 0.11 under the hood). The RustCrypto hashes
crate replaced type aliases with newtypes and moved compression internals
to a `block_api` module. The single consumer in this workspace
(crates/ffs-harness/tests/conformance.rs) uses only the stable
Digest-trait surface:

    let mut hasher = Sha256::new();
    hasher.update(bytes);
    let digest = hasher.finalize();

That surface is unchanged between 0.10 and 0.11, so no code edits were
required. SHA-256 output is byte-identical (the whole point of the
standardized hash), so any on-disk fixture checksums remain valid.

Transitive sha2 0.10.9 (pulled via older deps) was purged from the
lockfile; now only sha2 0.11.0 + digest 0.11 are present.

Verification:
  - rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets: clean
  - rch exec -- cargo test -p ffs-harness --lib: 312 passed / 0 failed
  - conformance.rs integration suite: 66 pass / 4 pre-existing failures
    (fixture_checksum_manifest_is_complete, ext4_e2compr_write_readback_*,
    btrfs_tree_block_checksum_tamper_detection_*, full_conformance_gate_pass)
    These 4 fail identically before and after this bump — they are
    pre-existing fixture-drift issues owned by other ongoing work, not
    caused by sha2 0.11.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
